query: Build an immersive WebGL portfolio site with scroll-driven 3D scenes and particle effects. It needs to be accessible — some visitors may have vestibular disorders or photosensitive epilepsy. Make sure it works for everyone.
assertions: Announces it is using the immersive-frontend skill
Addresses vestibular/motion sensitivity with specific mitigation strategies
Addresses seizure/photosensitivity risks (flash frequency limits, contrast thresholds)
Provides a prefers-reduced-motion implementation that goes beyond just disabling animations
Specifies a meaningful static fallback for users with reduced-motion enabled
Mentions cognitive overload considerations (not overwhelming with simultaneous effects)
Provides concrete code patterns for the accessibility implementations, not just guidelines

query: I have an existing Next.js e-commerce site built with shadcn/ui. I want to add an immersive 3D product showcase section — scroll-driven with the product rotating and exploding into parts. But the rest of the site stays as standard UI. How do I scope this?
assertions: Announces it is using the immersive-frontend skill
Recognizes this as a hybrid project (immersive section within standard UI)
Provides scope partitioning guidance — which parts use immersive-frontend vs frontend-design
Addresses canvas containment (scoped canvas within a section, not full-page)
Covers the intersection of shadcn/ui components with the WebGL section
Provides guidance on loading strategy (lazy-load the 3D section to avoid blocking the UI)
Addresses scroll behavior handoff between standard DOM scrolling and the immersive section

query: Build a canvas-heavy agency landing page with WebGL background effects and custom shaders. It needs to rank well on Google (SEO matters) and I need to verify it runs at 60fps on mid-range devices. How do I profile and optimize?
assertions: Announces it is using the immersive-frontend skill
Provides SEO guidance specific to canvas-heavy sites (semantic HTML beneath canvas, meta tags, structured data)
Addresses crawlability — ensuring content is in the DOM, not only rendered on canvas
Provides a concrete performance profiling workflow using Chrome DevTools
Mentions specific DevTools panels/tabs to use (Performance, Layers, Rendering)
Provides guidance on identifying GPU bottlenecks vs CPU bottlenecks
References the performance budgets from the skill (draw calls, triangle count, texture memory)
